name: golang-github-go-logfmt-logfmt-dev
title: golang-github-go-logfmt-logfmt-dev
description:
Marshals and unmarshals logfmt messages
Package logfmt implements utilities to marshal and unmarshal data in the
logfmt format (https://brandur.org/logfmt). It provides an API similar
to encoding/json (http://golang.org/pkg/encoding/json/) and encoding/xml
(http://golang.org/pkg/encoding/xml/).
.
The logfmt format has not been formally standardized. The most authoritative
public specification to date has been the documentation of a Go Language
package (http://godoc.org/github.com/kr/logfmt) written by Blake Mizerany and
Keith Rarick.
.
This project attempts to conform as closely as possible to the prior art,
while also removing ambiguity where necessary to provide well behaved encoder
and decoder implementations.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use golang-github-go-logfmt-logfmt-dev because Marshals and unmarshals logfmt messages.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use golang-github-go-logfmt-logfmt-dev as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair golang-github-go-logfmt-logfmt-dev with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `golang-github-go-logfmt-logfmt-dev --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
